definition: The problem of deciding whether an arbitrary program halts on an arbitrary input.
A program stops execution by returning accepting rejecting erroring or otherwise terminating.
A program continues indefinitely rather than halting.
A procedure can confirm yes cases but may not halt on no cases.
No general decider exists for all valid inputs.
A program or expression refers to its own description.
A construction that contradicts a proposed complete decider or enumeration.
A theorem that nontrivial semantic properties of programs are undecidable in general.
A result that is not decided within scope or resource limits.
